description: Percentage of initial reads remaining after quality trimming and filtering
Rationale/reference: Losing more than 30% of reads indicates poor quality (as long as filters are set correctly)

description: Percentage of initial reads remaining after quality trimming and filtering
Rationale/reference: Losing more than 30% of reads indicates poor quality (as long as filters are set correctly)

description: Alignment rate of processed reads
Rationale/reference: Higher alignment rates indicate high quality reads

description: Alignment rate of processed reads
Rationale/reference: Higher alignment rates indicate high quality reads

description: Percentage of reference genome masked by combined mask
Rationale/reference: Even if only masking germline variants, percent masked should be at least 0.1%

description: Difference in the percentage of total read pairs that belong to each sample
Rationale/reference: If products are correctly formed in all samples, the difference in total reads within the pool should be small

description: Percentage of total adaptors after demux that are contaminants
Rationale/reference: 0.0001% represents background levels, inclusion of 1 contaminating adaptor prior to PCR would result in ~0.0004%

description: Ratio of products that have adapters, sample indices, UMIs, and R1 and R2 in the expected order
Rationale/reference: Based on correct product ratio observed in CODECseq paper

description: Coverage in the analysable genome by duplex (assuming 250M 150PE reads)
Rationale/reference: 0.5x ensures 1x depth across entire analysable genome

description: Tally of the total number of variants called (x10^6  variants)
Rationale/reference: Does the number of called variants agrees with the number of expected germline variants in the population (TABLE 1: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC9439720/)

description: Calculate the ratio of SNV's / INDELS
Rationale/reference: Does the SNV/INDEL ratio agree with the expected population ratio (TABLE 1: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC9439720/)

description: Assess whether the IN/DEL ratio agress with the literature
Rationale/reference: TABLE 1: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC9439720/

description: Number of MNP / OTHER variants after decomposition
Rationale/reference: MNP/OTHER should be converted to SNVs/INDELs

description: Assess whether the Ti/Tv ratio agrees with the expected ratio
Rationale/reference: https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1002/bimj.202300278

description: Assess whether the Het/Hom ratio agrees with the expected ratio
Rationale/reference: https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1002/bimj.202300278

description: Percentage of reads that map to multiple genomic locations
Rationale/reference: Reads should almost all map to only one position

description: Percentage of reads that map to multiple genomic locations

description: Percentage of reads marked as duplicates
Rationale/reference: Duplication rates are typically ~3-7% with PCR-free prep

description: Percentage of library that is flagged as UMI duplicates
Rationale/reference: Based on duplication rate observed in CODECseq paper

description: Peak of insert size distribution as determined by alignment (bp)
Rationale/reference: Illumina PCR-free prep should yield mean insert size 350-550bp depending on size selection

description: Mean insert size as determined by alignment (bp)
Rationale/reference: 150bp gives ideal overlap, 300 gives no overlap and 50 gives 50bp overlap

description: Total number of reads obtained from standard Illumina sequencing by AGRF (x million reads)
Rationale/reference: AGRF sequences to 100Gb or ~333 million read pairs
